items.title: 'Similar to a blender': Gina Rinehart defends helipad amid noise concerns

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart wants to be able to land helicopters on the roof of her corporate HQ, and says the noise experienced by nearby residents would be similar to using a coffee machine or blender.

articles.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart

gallery.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles as she is interviewed by a journalist.

items.title: Court hears doctor unknowingly communicated with self-described paedophile vigilante

children.content: A Perth paediatrician alleged to have sexually assaulted child patients was unknowingly communicating online with a self-described paedophile vigilante, a court has heard.

image.altText: A head and shoulders shot of an Asian-Australian man, with black hair, wearing spectacles and a blue and white business shirt.

items.title: Suspected bird flu cases detected in NSW and WA after devastating impact overseas

children.content: A suspected positive case of H5 bird flu has been identified in New South Wales, the state agriculture minister has announced.

image.altText: NSW Bennetts Beach where the first suspected case of H5 bird flu was found

items.title: As WA school holidays loom, fears mount for road safety

children.content: With fatalities on WA roads on track to surpass their highest toll in almost two decades, first responders fear the upcoming school holidays will make things worse.

image.altText: Old school building across the road with two cars moving quickly in front

items.title: Former AFL great Nicky Winmar found guilty of assaulting woman

children.content: The woman previously told the court she was "fearful" for her life on the night of the incident last year.

image.altText: Nicky wearing a grey coat and sunglasses.

items.title: 'Grieving best mate' of West Coast Eagles star pens open letter to AFL

children.content: In a five-page letter to the executives and commissioners of the AFL, a grieving friend slams the league for "failing" former Eagles premiership player Adam Hunter, who suffered from CTE.

items.title: If the odds are anything to go by, this 104yo WWII veteran should not be alive

children.content: Eighty five years ago, Brian Walley, a prisoner of war in a German camp after crashing his bomber into the North Sea, doubted he would live for long. Yesterday, he celebrated his 104th birthday.

image.altText: A close up of a smiling man with grey hair, a beard and dark rectangular glasses.

items.title: Community leaders back Coles supermarket rejection in Kalgoorlie-Boulder

children.content: Welcoming a landmark decision by the consumer watchdog, Kalgoorlie-Boulder's peak business group says the city's 30,000-strong population does not justify the development of another supermarket.

image.altText: A female business executive wearing a suit jacket.

items.title: Thousands more Perth properties to be subdivided under major shake-up

children.content: Major planning changes will allow more Perth home owners to subdivide their properties in a bid to boost housing supply as the city continues to fall short of its infill target.

items.title: 'Immense breach of human rights': WA strip searches double in three years

children.content: The number of people strip searched by police officers in Western Australia more than doubles in three years, leading some to label the practice a human rights violation.

image.altText: Exterior of Perth Police Centre, Sept 2024

items.title: 'Deadly' Indigenous remote artists travel to Sydney

children.content: Remote Indigenous artists from around Australia are in Sydney for the annual National Indigenous Art Fair.

image.altText: Woman putting up an artwork in the exhibition.

items.title: 'Advocate for yourself': Woman's warning after partner's cancer death

children.content: Rory Esler's family and friends will take part in the Gold Coast marathon festival this weekend to honour his memory and to raise awareness, encouraging young Australians to advocate for themselves when navigating the medical system.

image.altText: woman sitting on couch in home

items.title: No mice found after drone baiting on remote WA island

children.content: A world-first drone-baiting program to protect migratory bird populations shows early signs of success off Western Australia's north coast.

items.title: Friday news quiz: A space rescue, high-profile pranks and the importance of using your full posterior

items.title: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt?

children.content: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt and win their first ever knockout final at a World Cup?
The Pharaohs have Salah, Marmoush and a dangerous counterattack, but the Socceroos have the physical edge, aerial threats and set-piece power to cause serious problems.
We take a closer look Egypt's strengths, and why Australia are a dangerous underdog that shouldn't be written off.

items.title: Jewish community frustrated by delays to social media duty of care

items.title: Australian in Israeli Defence Force committed war crimes, report alleges

children.content: The Australian Federal Police is considering a brief of evidence from the Australian Centre for International Justice, detailing alleged war crimes in Gaza by an Australian citizen who was in the Israeli Defence Force.

items.title: Mining company fined after worker trapped under coal for eight hours

children.content: The bulldozer was trapped inside a coal valve at a mine site in Central Queensland for eight hours. The mine's owner has now been fined more than $130,000.

image.altText: A pile of coal, with a small bit of catepillar tracks sticking up the top

items.title: As they mourn their daughter, family wants a ‘life for a life’ from accused man

children.content: The family of a Thai teenager allegedly killed by a man from Western Australia places her cremated ashes to rest at a local Buddhist temple as they seek justice for her murder.

articles.title: Photos show room where police allege Thai teenager was last seen

children.content: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

image.altText: A woman speaks to a man in a room.

articles.title: Man accused of Thai girl's murder grew up and owned firearms in WA, moved eight months ago

children.content: Australian Simon Peter Carman went about "normal life", going out, washing his clothes and talking to neighbours after allegedly murdering a teenage girl and stuffing her body inside a suitcase in Thailand, police say.

image.altText: A man sits in a singlet behind a desk looking at the camera

gallery.altText: a gold urn in a concrete nook with white flowers infront of the urn

thumbnail.altText: a gold urn in a concrete nook with white flowers infront of the urn
Simon has back to camera, wheels a suitcase, grainy photo, tiled hallway, wears black sleeveless top, shorts.
